comment.body:
As market could come through these last two days without spot up/vol up kind of squeeze, and spot/vol beta was sticky up only towards the downside, and finally broke my premarket marked lvl, we can say that bear side is done.
If the deal is done, and probabilities are high, it will boost the market on the short-run, but as said, it is only to pull fresh liquidity in for further downside into the post-OpEx vacuum.
Please, take your time to revisit, re-read my last three analysis in order to make it clear, and lets discuss here.

comment.body:
I want to warn you however, that even tho my bias is bottom since we hit 5532 on Tuesday, be hedged!
Why? Bcs shorter skew, shorter vol trades make the market exposed to violent vol squeeze if a geopolitical surprise happens.
Again: understand it. It's not about the Fed, it is about geos... Fed is also manipulated by the gov to do what they want to do, they have every tools for that.
No need to over-hedged, but don't be nakedly exposed, and complacent!

user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
Larry Williams on StockCharts TV - "Rally Time."


**Reasons for a Potential Rally:**

* **Undervaluation:** Williams believes the market is currently undervalued. He uses his "Will Value" indicator (available on StockCharts.com) to identify periods when the market is undervalued, which he sees as buying opportunities.
* **Smart Money Buying:** He observes that "smart money" (professionals) has started buying stocks, as indicated by his Money Flow Index. He sees this as a positive sign.
* **Advisory Sentiment Index:** Williams tracks the sentiment of investment advisors. He notes that when the majority of advisors are bearish (pessimistic), it often signals a potential buying opportunity. Currently, he sees bearish sentiment among advisors.
* **Cyclical and Seasonal Factors:** He uses cyclical studies and seasonal patterns to suggest that the timing is favorable for a rally.

**Factors Influencing the Market:**

* **Tariffs:** Williams discusses the potential impact of tariffs, suggesting they could increase prices but also increase jobs. He believes that the impact on inflation could be offset by increased domestic oil production.
* **Crude Oil:** He emphasizes the strong relationship between crude oil prices and inflation. He suggests that if "drill baby drill" policies are implemented, increased oil production could lead to lower inflation, which would be positive for the market.
* **Relationship between Crude Oil and Stock Prices:** Williams highlights the historical correlation between crude oil prices and stock prices.

**Stocks to Consider:**

Williams mentions several stocks and sectors that he finds interesting:

* **Tesla (TSLA):** He notes that smart money is buying Tesla, the stock is undervalued, and the seasonal pattern is turning bullish.
* **Consumer Staples ETF (XLP):** He likes this sector because it may be less influenced by tariffs. He sees a potential low coming for XLP and a favorable probability of a rally.
* **NVIDIA (NVDA):** He observes that NVIDIA has become undervalued and professional money is buying it again.
* **ASML Holding N.V. (ASML):** He suggests this stock has held up better than NVIDIA.
* **Apple (AAPL):** He notes that Apple has held up comparatively well and is at a cyclical point for a potential rally.

**What Could Ignite the Rally?**

Williams speculates that a potential resolution of the war in Ukraine, possibly through negotiations facilitated by Donald Trump, could be the trigger to ignite a rally.

In summary, Larry Williams is presenting his analysis and reasons for believing that the stock market is poised for a rally, based on a combination of valuation, sentiment, cyclical, and fundamental factors.

comment.body:
The US allies in the MENA region are Israel and the Gulf monarchies, e.g., Saudi & UAE, all of which favor Trump. Saudi and Emirati money should also be the ones that will buy into the tech sectors. A potential US-Russia-Israel-Gulf alliance would be one based on the shared idea of the fundamental role of religion, rivaling "woke liberalism" and Marxism (political or cultural).

Now the Gulf monarchies are not hawkish towards China and do maintain decent relations. Yet, the US is the guarantor of the sheikhs' thrones and wealth, whereas China a tacit backer of Iran and its export of revolutions.

comment.body: NVDA was down $1.25T on friday from the highs. I think Fwd earnings PE dipped under 20, seems very cheap given its growth, still everything can get cheaper. TRIN droped from 0.9 to 0.53 in the last 10mins of trade. So all the volume in the big late reports from dark pools at the close was in names which where up on the day. This forces the TRIN down

comment.body:
Oh, and one more thing: never get discouraged if you don’t understand one of my analyses the first time, or even the second time.
 I can assure you that most of them are the result of many hours or even days of thinking, backed by over 10+ yrs of serious and compelx experience and hard studies. 
As Euclid said, there is no royal road. 
You reap what you sow. Never forget that!

comment.body:
Here is my homework:

1. If Thursday was the bottom, then until 3/21 OPEX:
1.1 Moderate case:
Spot rises, vol skew flattens, and vol declines slowly. The liquidation issue gets resolved. Vanna/Charm flows support the price since we are within JPM Collar's range.
1.2 Extreme case:
Spot surges, vol skew flattens significantly. Vol increases if people rush to buy OTM calls (we’ll also see huge volume there). This case needs some catalyst, probably the FOMC.

2. If Thursday was not the bottom and Monday continues to fall: 
Big players will likely act as they did this week—adding liquidity to suppress vol and ultimately prevent a deeper crash. Price oscillates. Mean reversion trading chances.

Here is where I see it as being useful of ra potential trade on the 15m SPX chart going into afternoon. The yellow line is the mean that was reverted to, and given how it lined up with broader price action and 5610 as a strong gamma level it was a good R:R entry for a long to again test the higher end of the channel(s)? comment.body:
Unsure how are you seeing the chat as it says for paid members only? But yes I think you may have to subscribe on a desktop. Substack mobile doesn’t allow - perhaps due to Apple App Store 30% cut on all subscriptions in-app

Or you may be able to subscribe via mobile browser on substack. Not the app though
